compact_summary:
<analysis>
The conversation consists of a series of explicit user requests followed by a final request for a comprehensive text-only summary. Let me analyze chronologically:

Message 1: User requested reading file1.txt and stopping. I used the Read tool, received the file content (alpha on line 1), and provided visible output.

Message 2: User feedback that my previous response had no visible output. This was regarding the file1.txt response - the user wanted me to explicitly show what was in the file. I then provided visible output showing "Line 1: alpha" and "Line 2: (empty)".

Message 3: User requested reading file2.txt and stopping. I used the Read tool and received content (beta on line 1) and provided output showing the file contents.

Message 4: User requested reading file3.txt and stopping. I used the Read tool and received content (gamma on line 1). I provided the tool result but didn't show explicit visible output.

Message 5 (current): User is requesting a detailed text-only summary of the entire conversation with explicit instructions NOT to use any tools. The user emphasizes this is CRITICAL and that tool calls will be REJECTED and waste the only turn.

The pattern is clear: simple sequential file read operations followed by a summary request.
</analysis>

<summary>
